audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim priestly support, prohibited divination, prophet-like-Moses, and false-prophet law, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Levite portions, mobile Levite service, child sacrifice, divination and necromancy prohibitions, prophet like Moses, Horeb mediation, prophetic accountability, and failed-word test.

verses.restored: Levitical priests, all tribe of Levi, shall have no portion or inheritance with Israel; fire-offerings of YHWH and his inheritance they shall eat.
No inheritance shall be for him in midst of his brothers; YHWH is his inheritance, as he spoke to him.
This shall be judgment/due of priests from people, from those sacrificing sacrifice, whether ox or sheep: one shall give priest shoulder, two cheeks, and stomach.
First of your grain, new wine, and oil, and first of fleece of your flock you shall give him.
For him YHWH your Divine chose from all your tribes to stand to serve in name of YHWH, he and his sons all days.
If Levite comes from one of your gates out of all Israel where he sojourns, and comes with all desire of his soul/life to place YHWH chooses,
then he shall serve in name of YHWH his Divine like all his brothers, Levites who stand there before YHWH.
Portion like portion they shall eat, besides what comes from sale of ancestral property.
When you enter land YHWH your Divine gives you, you shall not learn to do according to abominations of those nations.
There shall not be found among you one who makes his son or daughter pass through fire, one practicing divination, soothsayer, omen-reader, sorcerer,
one binding spells, one consulting ghost or familiar spirit, or one seeking the dead.
For everyone doing these is an abomination to YHWH, and because of these abominations YHWH your Divine dispossesses them from before you.
Wholehearted/complete you shall be with YHWH your Divine.
For these nations whom you are dispossessing listen to soothsayers and diviners; but as for you, YHWH your Divine has not given you so.
A prophet from your midst, from your brothers, like me, YHWH your Divine will raise up for you; to him you shall listen.
According to all you asked from YHWH your Divine at Horeb on day of assembly, saying, Let me not continue hearing voice of YHWH my Divine, and this great fire let me not see again, lest I die.
YHWH said to me, They have done well in what they spoke.
A prophet I will raise up for them from midst of their brothers, like you; I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all I command him.
It shall be, the man who does not listen to my words that he speaks in my name, I myself will require it from him.
But prophet who acts presumptuously to speak a word in my name that I did not command him to speak, or who speaks in name of other gods, that prophet shall die.
If you say in your heart, How shall we know word YHWH did not speak?
When prophet speaks in name of YHWH and thing does not happen or come, that is word YHWH did not speak; in presumption prophet spoke it. You shall not be afraid of him.

translation_notes: Levitical dependence creates communal obligations of material support and equal access to service.
The mobile Levite may join central service according to desire and receive an equal portion.
The divination list reflects ancient Israelite boundaries around authorized revelation; it must not be used to persecute harmless spiritual, cultural, psychological, or religious practices today.
The prophet like Moses arises from within Israel and belongs first to Jewish covenant interpretation; later claims must not erase that context.
Fulfillment is one test of prophecy, but Deuteronomy 13 also insists that signs alone are insufficient.
The death penalty for false prophecy is source-preserved and categorically barred from modern enforcement.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 18 to execute alleged false prophets, persecute minority religions, criminalize harmless spiritual practice, or appropriate the prophet-like-Moses promise against Jewish interpretation. Center protection of children, material support for servants of community, accountable speech, and freedom from fear of failed predictions.
